FTAI.headline: Something Amazing That No One Predicted Just Happened in the Markets (Yes, AI Is at the Center of It)
FTAI.description: GE Vernova surpassed GE Aerospace in market capitalization for the first time, driven by surging AI data center demand for its gas turbines. The HA turbine received over four times more orders in 2025 than in 2018, with both companies benefiting from strong management and technological advantages. GE Vernova raised full-year guidance due to booming orders, while GE Aerospace faces headwinds from the Iran conflict.

PWR.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones Roars As Industrials Rescue Wall Street From Meta-Led Tech Slide
PWR.description: The Dow Jones surged 1.5% driven by industrial and healthcare stocks, while mega-cap tech faced pressure from concerns over AI capital spending. Meta and Microsoft declined on capex worries, but Alphabet gained on enterprise AI wins. Industrials like Caterpillar, Quanta Services, and Carrier rallied on data-center infrastructure demand. The S&P 500 gained 0.5% while the Nasdaq 100 remained flat.

PWR.headline: Quanta Services Shares Rally On Record Infrastructure Demand
PWR.description: Quanta Services (PWR) shares surged 14.59% after reporting a strong Q1 beat with adjusted EPS of $2.68 (vs. $2.06 consensus) and $7.9B in revenue (vs. $7B expected). The company raised FY26 guidance to $34.70-$35.20B revenue and $13.55-$14.25 adjusted EPS, driven by record $48.5B backlog reflecting robust infrastructure demand across utility and generation markets.

QCOM.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones Roars As Industrials Rescue Wall Street From Meta-Led Tech Slide
QCOM.description: The Dow Jones surged 1.5% driven by industrial and healthcare stocks, while mega-cap tech faced pressure from concerns over AI capital spending. Meta and Microsoft declined on capex worries, but Alphabet gained on enterprise AI wins. Industrials like Caterpillar, Quanta Services, and Carrier rallied on data-center infrastructure demand. The S&P 500 gained 0.5% while the Nasdaq 100 remained flat.

QCOM.headline: S&P 500 Resilience Persists Even as Underlying Momentum Fades
QCOM.description: The S&P 500 is consolidating after recent tech earnings, showing resilience despite headwinds including rising oil prices, higher bond yields, and a hawkish Fed stance that has eliminated rate cut expectations. While the index is holding support levels, momentum has stalled and the rally appears increasingly dependent on AI-driven institutional flows into mega-cap tech stocks. Technical consolidation between 7000-7043 suggests a pause rather than a reversal.

QCOM.headline: Why Is Qualcomm Stock Soaring Thursday?
QCOM.description: Qualcomm shares surged 10.96% in premarket trading after reporting Q2 earnings that beat expectations with $10.60B revenue and $2.65 EPS. However, the company issued weaker-than-expected Q3 guidance. The stock rally was driven by strong automotive segment growth (surpassing $5B annual run rate), progress in agentic AI initiatives, and data center expansion plans, though near-term headwinds from memory supply constraints persist.

QCOM.headline: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan Just Delivered Phenomenal News to Shareholders
QCOM.description: Intel reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with data center revenue surging 22% to over $5 billion. CEO Lip-Bu Tan highlighted that CPUs are becoming increasingly critical to AI infrastructure as the orchestration layer for agentic AI systems, with the GPU-to-CPU ratio shifting from 8-to-1 toward parity. This positions Intel to capitalize on its decades of CPU expertise and vertical integration in manufacturing.

TTMI.headline: Why TTM Technologies Stock Soared More Than 15% Higher Today
TTMI.description: TTM Technologies stock surged over 15% after reporting record Q1 2026 results with net sales up 30% year-over-year to $846 million and non-GAAP net income up 52% to $0.75 per share, both significantly exceeding analyst expectations. The company attributed strong performance to sustained demand from data center and networking markets driven by AI infrastructure expansion. Q2 guidance also beat analyst estimates, positioning TTM as a beneficiary of long-term AI infrastructure growth.

VIAV.headline: Why Viavi Solutions Stock Triumphed on Thursday
VIAV.description: Viavi Solutions stock surged over 15% after beating Q3 earnings estimates with $407M in revenue (43% YoY growth) and exceeding guidance expectations. The company's strong performance is driven by thriving demand in data center and aerospace industries, with optimistic outlook for continued growth in both sectors.

VIAV.headline: Viavi Stock Is Trending Overnight: Here's What You Should Know
VIAV.description: Viavi Solutions (NASDAQ: VIAV) shares surged 25.17% after-hours following strong Q3 results that beat earnings estimates by 22.73% and revenue by 3.32%. The company reported net revenue of $406.8 million (up 42.8% YoY) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 (up 80% YoY), driven by growth in data center and aerospace/defense markets. Bullish Q4 guidance projecting revenue of $427-$437 million and EPS of $0.29-$0.31 further boosted investor confidence.

SFM.headline: Why Sprouts Farmers Market Is Rocketing Higher Today
SFM.description: Sprouts Farmers Market stock surged 18% after delivering Q1 earnings that exceeded Wall Street's low expectations, with 4% sales growth and guidance for 5.5% full-year revenue growth. Despite a challenging consumer environment and stock decline over the past year, the company's expansion plans, including its first New York location, and valuation at 15x forward earnings drove investor optimism.

GNRC.headline: Oil Surges 6%, S&P 500 Wobbles Before Fed And Tech Earnings: Stock Market Today
GNRC.description: U.S. stocks traded mixed on Wednesday as crude oil rallied 5-6% amid Iran tensions, pushing Treasury yields above 4.4%. The Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates steady at 3.75%, while investors await earnings from major tech companies. Big Tech stocks fell 1-2% on reports that OpenAI missed revenue targets, raising questions about AI spending sustainability.

SIMO.headline: Silicon Motion Announces Results for the Quarterly Period Ended March 31, 2026
SIMO.description: Silicon Motion Technology Corporation reported exceptional first quarter 2026 results with net sales of $342.1 million, up 23% sequentially and 105% year-over-year. The company exceeded expectations driven by strong growth in eMMC & UFS controllers (up 140-145% Y/Y) and Ferri & Boot Drive solutions (up 755-760% Y/Y), though SSD controller sales declined 5-10% Q/Q. For Q2 2026, the company projects revenue of $393-411 million with continued sequential and year-over-year growth.

INTC.headline: Nvidia vs. Intel: Diverging Paths in Quarterly Revenue
INTC.description: Nvidia demonstrates substantially stronger revenue performance than Intel, posting eight consecutive quarters of rising revenue with a 73% year-over-year increase in Q4 2024, while Intel's revenue has remained largely flat. Despite Intel's recent 7% year-over-year growth in Q1 2025—its highest in eight quarters—Nvidia's trailing-12-month revenue of $188 billion dwarfs Intel's $53 billion. Both companies are benefiting from AI demand, but Nvidia's GPU dominance and higher profit margins position

INTC.headline: Microsoft Earnings Look Strong, But Investors Focus on Risks
INTC.description: Microsoft delivered strong Q3 2026 earnings with 40% Azure growth and $37 billion in AI revenue, but shares fell 5% post-earnings due to investor concerns over $190 billion annual capex spending and OpenAI relationship risks. Despite solid fundamentals and a 37% upside from consensus price targets, market sentiment remains cautious about potential AI bubble and OpenAI revenue sustainability.

INTC.headline: Bay Area Students Raise the Roof with Engineering Solutions at The Tech Interactive's 39th Annual Tech Challenge
INTC.description: More than 1,100 students from Northern California participated in The Tech Interactive's 39th annual Tech Challenge, designing and building innovative lifting devices to address affordable housing construction. The event showcased student engineering skills, creativity, and problem-solving abilities, with special recognition given to teams demonstrating exceptional collaboration, documentation, and real-world application of engineering principles.

INTC.headline: Intel's 115% Stock Boom Is Shattering Records After Years Of Doubt
INTC.description: Intel stock surged approximately 115% in April, closing at $94.75 after starting the month at $44.13, marking a record-breaking rally and dramatic turnaround. The comeback reflects improved execution under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, stronger data center demand, and renewed optimism around Intel's foundry strategy. The U.S. government's investment in Intel, authorized by President Trump, has generated over $30 billion in gains. While risks remain regarding Intel's foundry business competitiveness, the marke

INTC.headline: Oil Surges 6%, S&P 500 Wobbles Before Fed And Tech Earnings: Stock Market Today
INTC.description: U.S. stocks traded mixed on Wednesday as crude oil rallied 5-6% amid Iran tensions, pushing Treasury yields above 4.4%. The Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates steady at 3.75%, while investors await earnings from major tech companies. Big Tech stocks fell 1-2% on reports that OpenAI missed revenue targets, raising questions about AI spending sustainability.

INTC.headline: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan Just Delivered Phenomenal News to Shareholders
INTC.description: Intel reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with data center revenue surging 22% to over $5 billion. CEO Lip-Bu Tan highlighted that CPUs are becoming increasingly critical to AI infrastructure as the orchestration layer for agentic AI systems, with the GPU-to-CPU ratio shifting from 8-to-1 toward parity. This positions Intel to capitalize on its decades of CPU expertise and vertical integration in manufacturing.

INTC.headline: Prediction: AI Stocks Will Soar After This Earnings Season and Here's Why
INTC.description: AI stocks, which faced headwinds earlier in 2026 due to high valuations and geopolitical concerns, are poised to rebound strongly during earnings season. Recent positive earnings reports from semiconductor and cloud companies, combined with improved geopolitical conditions and mounting evidence of sustained AI demand, suggest the AI growth story is real and durable.

INTC.headline: Why Is Everyone Talking About Intel Stock Right Now?
INTC.description: Intel is experiencing positive momentum as it reaps the benefits of years of substantial investments. The management team expects favorable performance to continue into the next year, driving recent stock gains and investor interest in the semiconductor company.

INTC.headline: Initta Technology's "Infinity" Pavilion at 2026 CHINASHOP Signals a New Era for AI-Driven, Open, and Sustainable Retail
INTC.description: Initta Technology showcased its AI-driven smart retail solutions at CHINASHOP 2026 in Hangzhou, presenting a comprehensive product matrix including self-service terminals and intelligent cashier devices powered by its S-AIoT platform. The company attracted significant international interest and secured multiple preliminary cooperation agreements, with Intel executives engaging in strategic discussions about technology enablement and retail innovation.

INTC.headline: Why the "Great Repricing" of AI Software Stocks Is a Gift for Long-Term Investors
INTC.description: Software stocks like Atlassian, C3.ai, and SoundHound AI have dropped over 50% in six months due to fears that all-in-one AI platforms will make traditional software obsolete. However, the article argues this represents a buying opportunity, as these companies are integrating AI into their offerings and possess durable competitive advantages that general-purpose chatbots cannot easily replicate.

BE.headline: Meet the Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Beating the S&P 500, the Nasdaq-100, and the Dow Jones in 2026
BE.description: The Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF is outperforming major U.S. indexes in 2026 with an 11.1% return year-to-date. Small-cap companies in the Russell 2000 are more insulated from geopolitical risks as they conduct most business domestically, while large-cap tech-heavy indexes face headwinds from global instability. The Russell's diversified sector composition and exposure to beneficiaries of U.S. government policies position it to continue outperforming in 2026.

BE.headline: Stock Market Today, April 29: Mixed Reactions as Big Tech Earnings Beat Expectations
BE.description: On April 29, 2026, major stock indices remained flat as the Fed held rates unchanged amid elevated inflation concerns. Big Tech earnings after hours showed mixed results: Alphabet and Amazon gained on strong performance, while Meta and Microsoft fell. The market is focused on AI spending pressures, with tech companies' combined capex exceeding $650 billion as they race to dominate the AI market.

BE.headline: This Hydrogen Stock Is Soaring on Earnings. The Iran Blockade Could Give It a Lasting Tailwind
BE.description: Bloom Energy surged following strong Q1 2026 earnings with 130% YoY revenue growth and beat profit expectations. The company is well-positioned to benefit from elevated oil prices resulting from the Iran blockade, as businesses and consumers seek alternative energy solutions. With major partnerships like Oracle's 2.8 GW deployment expansion, Bloom appears positioned for growth even as geopolitical uncertainty persists.

BE.headline: Is FuelCell Energy (FCEL) the Next Bloom Energy?
BE.description: FuelCell Energy is pivoting to compete for lucrative data center contracts with a new 12.5 MW Power Block offering, capitalizing on surging demand from AI hyperscalers. While the company's business pipeline has grown 275% since February 2025 and it plans to triple production capacity, it faces significant execution risks and capacity constraints compared to competitor Bloom Energy, which already has $20 billion in backlog and secured major deals.

BE.headline: Is Oracle Undervalued as Cloud Growth Accelerates?
BE.description: Oracle's stock has been undervalued despite its successful transition to cloud infrastructure and hyperscale data center operations. The company has contracted revenue from major clients like Alphabet and Amazon to build data centers, with plans to nearly double its current 160 data center footprint. Trading at 23X 2026 forecasts with potential 400-700% upside by 2033, Oracle shows strong technical rebound signals with institutional accumulation and positive analyst sentiment ahead of Q4 earning
